title: The 50K Showdown — TPT Test vs FFN Steady vs TradeDay Quick Pay
excerpt: Same account size, three different rulebooks. Targets, drawdowns, consistency math, buffers, and payout mechanics — so you know exactly what your money buys.
eyebrow: Sire Mammat's Lab · Futures Prop Firm Intel · July 2026

content.heading: Same Eval, Different Endgame
content.body: On paper these plans are triplets: $3,000 target, $2,000 of room, zero daily loss limits, zero funded consistency. The separation happens the day you get funded — one plan's drawdown stops moving, and two flip you to intraday trailing where every unrealized run-up that retraces eats your cushion.

content.heading: Which 50K Fits You
content.body: Size + uncapped payouts → TPT Test: 6 minis / 60 micros and ~1-hour uncapped withdrawals; respect the intraday switch on PRO and the $649 PRO reset. Value + cleanest funded rules → FFN Steady: $115 buys two accounts, 2-day pass, and the only funded drawdown that goes static. Fastest cash out → TradeDay Quick Pay: no buffer, day-one withdrawals at $250 minimum; the 50/50 split under $4k is the toll.

content.heading: The Honest Caveats
content.body: TPT and TradeDay both flip funded accounts to intraday trailing. TradeDay's 30% eval consistency ($900/day cap) is the tightest here and will shape how you pass. FFN's friction is the $52,000 payout buffer and 4-mini cap. And regardless of which you pick: diversify across firms — never make one prop your only funding source.

verdict.heading: The Drawdown Decides This One
paragraphs: All three run the identical eval, so the separation happens the day you get funded: FFN Steady keeps EOD and then locks static at start +$100 once you clear the buffer. TPT and TradeDay Quick Pay both flip you to intraday trailing.

FFN Steady takes the crown — cheapest in ($57.50/account on the BOGO), fastest pass (2 days), one-time fee, and the cleanest funded mechanics of the three. TradeDay QP is the velocity play: no buffer, day-one withdrawals, if you can trade tight under intraday trailing and push past the $4k 50/50 zone quickly. TPT is the size-and-speed play: 6 minis, ~1-hour uncapped payouts, PRO+ at 90/10 — with NOFEE40 it's no longer the pricey funnel it once was, but the $649 PRO reset and 3-reset cap make funded mistakes expensive.

disclaimers: Futures trading involves substantial risk of loss and is not suitable for all investors. Funded accounts are simulated trading accounts unless a firm's live tier states otherwise; payouts are made under each firm's published terms. Plan data verified July 2026 against firm plan cards and help centers; FFN Steady pricing reflects the live 50% + instant-BOGO promotion. Prices, targets, and rules change frequently — always confirm directly with each firm before purchasing. Links may carry affiliate codes — the Lab may earn a commission at no cost to you.
